California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
March 19, 1990
John R. Shaw
city Attorney
32400 Paseo Adelanto
san Juan capistrano, cA 92675
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-119
Dear Mr. shaw:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
responsibi l ities of l.tr. James Erickson, member of the Pl-anning
Commission of the city of san Juan capistrano, under the conflict-
of-interest provisionS of the politicaf 
Reform Act (the rtaslrrl.1
ft confirms the tentative advice provided to you on February 22,
1990.
OUESTION
Pacific ioint Partners j,s a linited partnership developing a
project in San Juan capistrano. May ltr. Erickson participate in
decisions regarding the rezoning, sj-te plan review, and tentative
tract nap approvals for the project?
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit Mr. Erickson from participating in
the decisions regarding the Pacific Point Partners' project since,
based on the facts provided, it does not appear reasonably
foreseeabl-e that the decisions will have a financial effect on
l{r. Erickson, or on the 1aw firn in lrhich Mr. Erickson is a
partner.
1^ Governnent code Sectj.ons 81000-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
comrnission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. A1l" references to regulations are to Title 
?
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8oo . P,o. Box 8o? o sacramento cA 958o4-o8oz . (916) 3 z2.s66o


Our Fl■e No. A-90-■■9
Page 2
FACTS
Pacific Point Partners is a linited partnership developing a
project in the City of San Juan capistrano (the 'rcity'r). 
This
project is scheduled to come before the planning conmission for
approvals regarding rezoning, site plan review, and the tentative
tract map.
D1r. Erickson is a partner in a 1aw f irrn, Nossanan, Guthner,
Knox and Elliott 
(the rrlaw firm"). 
He has a less than 1o-percent
ownership interest in the law firm.
Neither ur. Erickson nor the law firm represents Pacific
Point Partners. Mr. Erickson and his 1aw firru do represent North
Peak Partners, a linited partnership developing property in the
Elsinore Area which is located a few niles froro the city. 
Pacific
Point Partners and North Peak Partners have different corporate
general partners, but these corporations have a conmon individual
shareholder of a significant portion if not all the shares of both
corporations.
ANALYSI S
Section 87100 prohibits any public official 
from making,
participating in, or using his official 
position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest. 
An official 
has a financial interest in
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a Eaterial financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public gen9ra11y, on the official 
or a nenber of his
i,nrnediate f auri ry , z or on :
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or nore.
***
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the reg'ular course of business on
terms avaj.lable to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or nore in value provided to,
2 An official,s 
trimmediate fanilytr includes his spouse and
dependent children. 
(Section 82029.)


Our Fi■e No. A-90-■■9
Page 3
received by or promised to the public official.
within 12 months prior to the time lrhen the
decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
Section 87103 (a) , (c) and (d) .
Mr. Erickson j,s a public official. 
(Section 82048. ) He has
a less than lo-percent interest in the 1as/ firm, presumabLy worth
nore than $1,000. In additj.on, the law firro is a source of income
to hin, presunably aggregating nore than $250 in the previous 12
nonths.3 Finally, Mr, Erickson is a partner in the 1aw firm.
Accordingly, Mr. Erickson may not participate in a decision if it
wilI have a reasonablv foreseeable and rnaterial financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the pubLic generally, on
Mr. Erickson, or on the 1aw f irro.
Foreseeabi l itv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantiaL likelihood that they wiJ.1 occur. To be
foreseeable, the effects of a decision nust be more than a mere
possibitity,' hohrever certainty is not required. 
(Dosrnev Cares v.
Downey Communitv Development com. (1987) 196 cal. App. 3d 983,
989-991; witt v. Morroe/ (1977) 7O Ca1 . App. 3d 817, 822i In re
Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198 (copy enclosed) . ) The Act seeks to
prevent more than actual conflicts of interest, it seeks to
prevent even the appearance of a possible conflict of interest.
(witt v. uorrow, EllpE!! at 823. )
Ilr. Erickson and the 1aw firn represent North Peak Partners.
Even through North Peak Partners and Pacific Point Partners nay
have a conmon individual shareholder who owns a significant
portion if not all of the shares of the corporations which are the
general partners for the two lirnited partnerships, there does not
appear to be a substantial likelihood that the decisions regarding
tfrl pacitic Point Project will have a financial effect on
Mr. Erickson, or on the Ia$, firm. 
Therefore, it does not appear
reasonably foreseeable that the decisions will have a financial
effect on Ur. Erickson, or on the law firn. 
Accordingly, the Act
does not prohibit Mr. Erickson from participating in the decisions
regarding the Pacific Point Partners, project.
3 Section 82030 defines income to incLude a pro-rata share
income of a business entity in which the officiaL owns a
lo-percent or greater interest. Since Mr. Erickson owns a
than 1o-percent interest in the law firrn, sources of income
Iaw firm are not deemed sources of incone to Mr. Erickson.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact ne at (916) 322-5901.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
